### Post by andrewsawyer on 2005-10-27
Hi everyone,

I have an Acer TravelMate C110 Tablet PC, and I have spent days trying to get my touchscreen to work, however I'm having no luck.  I have downloaded the linuxwacom source from SourceForge and recompiled my kernel with it added, however all that seemed to do was to kill my wireless!

Below is my complete xorg.conf file:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"GLcore"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver       "wacom"
	Identifier   "cursor"
	Option       "Device" "/dev/ttyS0"
	Option       "InputFashion" "Tablet"
	Option       "Name" "GRAPHIRE / INTUOS (SERIAL)"
	Option       "SendCoreEvents" "on"
	Option       "Type" "cursor"
	Option       "Mode" "absolute"
	Option       "Vendor" "WACOM"
	Option       "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver       "wacom"
	Identifier   "stylus"
	Option       "Device" "/dev/ttyS0"
	Option       "InputFashion" "Pen"
	Option       "Name" "GRAPHIRE / INTUOS Stylus (SERIAL)"
	Option       "Type" "stylus"
	Option       "Mode" "absolute"
	Option       "Vendor" "WACOM"
	Option       "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver       "wacom"
	Identifier   "eraser"
	Option       "Device" "/dev/ttyS0"
	Option       "InputFashion" "Eraser"
	Option       "Name" "GRAPHIRE / INTUOS Eraser (SERIAL)"
	Option       "Type" "eraser"
	Option       "Mode" "absolute"
	Option       "Vendor" "WACOM"
	Option       "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"

		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	InputDevice  "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice  "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice  "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection


```

and I have tried typing the following into the command prompt, with the following error:
```
andrew@sam:~$ sudo modprobe wacom
andrew@sam:~$ dmesg | grep wacom
[4294981.233000] usbcore: registered new driver wacom
[4294981.233000] drivers/usb/input/wacom.c: v1.40:USB Wacom Graphire and Wacom Intuos tablet driver
andrew@sam:~$ sudo setserial /dev/ttyS0 port 0x06f8 irq 6 autoconfigure
andrew@sam:~$ wacdump -f c100 -c serial /dev/ttyS0
WacomOpenTablet: Invalid argument
andrew@sam:~$

```

While booting, I notice that it mentions Wacom on the new splash screen, and it gives an ok message.  If someone could please give me any assistance as to what I might be able to try next to get it to work, it would be very much appreciated.

P.S. I'm currently running the default 686 kernel (as I like my wireless working!)

P.P.S. I've got the Acer Hotkeys working, however I have to open a command prompt and type 'sudo modprobe acerhk' everytime I reboot.  Is there any way I can get this load automatically?  From memory, it's loaded as a module into the kernel.

Thank you in andvance.

Andy

---

### Post by 23meg on 2005-10-27
You don't have to compile linuxwacom from scratch; it comes in the package xserver-xorg-input wacom. Just make sure that package is installed, along with the wacom-tools package, and test whether your system has recognized the digitizer with the wacdump utility that comes in wacom-tools. 

If you do get a sign of life in wacdump, you've got the hard work done; the rest is probably an xorg.conf issue. I'll paste the relevant sections of my working xorg.conf at the bottom of this post.

As for acerhk, adding it to your /etc/modules file will load it on startup.

```
 
Section "InputDevice"
    Driver        "wacom"
    Identifier    "cursor"
    Option        "Device"        "/dev/ttyS0"
    Option        "InputFashion" "Tablet"
    Option        "ForceDevice"    "ISDV4"
    Option        "Name" "GRAPHIRE / INTUOS (SERIAL)"
    Option        "SendCoreEvents" "on"
    Option        "Type"          "cursor"
    Option        "Mode"          "absolute" 
    Option        "Vendor" "WACOM"
    Option        "Speed"         "3.0"
    Option        "Threshold"     "1"
    Option        "Tilt"          "on"
    Option	     "Button2"	"3"
    #Option    "DebugLevel"    "10"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Driver        "wacom"
    Identifier    "stylus"
    Option        "Device"        "/dev/ttyS0"
    Option        "Type"          "stylus"
    Option        "InputFashion" "Pen"
    Option        "SendCoreEvents" "on"
    Option        "Name"          "GRAPHIRE / INTUOS Stylus (SERIAL)"
    Option        "ForceDevice"    "ISDV4"
    Option        "Mode"          "absolute"
    Option        "Vendor" "WACOM"
    Option        "Tilt"          "on"
    Option        "TiltInvert"    "on"
    Option        "Threshold"     "1"
    Option	     "Button2"	"3"
   #Option    "DebugLevel"    "10"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
 Driver       "wacom"
 Identifier   "eraser"
 Option       "Device" "/dev/ttyS0"
 Option       "InputFashion" "Eraser"
 Option       "Name" "GRAPHIRE / INTUOS Eraser (SERIAL)"
 Option       "Type" "eraser"
 Option       "Mode" "absolute"
 Option       "Vendor" "WACOM"
 Option       "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" 
 Option        "SendCoreEvents" "on"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice	"Alps Glidepoint" "CorePointer"
        InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

```

---

### Post by andrewsawyer on 2005-10-27
Thank you for the quick reply.

I have checked, and I have the package xserver-xorg-input-wacom installed, as well as the wacom-tools.  Unfortunately, testing with wacdump results in the following:
```

andrew@sam:~$ wacdump -f c100 -c serial /dev/ttyS0
WacomOpenTablet: Invalid argument

```

I get nothing from the tablet when moving the stylus across it from top corner to opposite corner, and scribbling across it.  From all the how-to's I've read, this should get it going - if not perfectly, then I should at least get something, however I might as well just be using a plain none-touchscreen!

Do you have any other ideas that I might be able to try?  And do you know what 'WacomOpenTablet: Invalid argument' might mean?

Thank you again.

P.S. thanks about the acerhk - all done now :-)

---

### Post by 23meg on 2005-10-27
Maybe the packaged driver is conflicting with your self-compiled driver? Remove both, reinstall the package and try again.

---

### Post by bb002 on 2005-10-30
you may want to try /dev/ttyS1 or higher.

these are the lines in my xorg.conf

Section "InputDevice"
Driver "wacom"
Identifier "cursor"
Option "Device" "/dev/ttyS1" # SERIAL ONLY
Option "Type" "cursor"
Option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" #TABLET PCS ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
Driver "wacom"
Identifier "stylus"
Option "Device" "/dev/ttyS1" # SERIAL ONLY
Option "Type" "stylus"
Option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" #TABLET PCS ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
Driver "wacom"
Identifier "eraser"
Option "Device" "/dev/ttyS1" # SERIAL ONLY
Option "Type" "eraser"
Option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" #TABLET PCS ONLY
EndSection

And my pc is using the default driver that came with xorg. I just had to add these lines.


[edit]
My pc is a Gateway M275 but the tablet screen is seen as a "Serial C110 Tablet Device".

---

### Post by bb002 on 2005-10-30
in a terminal "sudo gedit  /etc/modules" and add "acerhk" at the end of the file. Now your hotkeys should get loaded automatically for now on.

Does the wacdump utility launch after you get that error message?

Did you launch wacdump with the /dev/ttyS1 argument this time? What happens if you try with ttys0? If you changed to ttys1 just in xorg.conf remember that you should change it in your setserial command as well.

### Post by 23meg on 2005-10-31
I highly suspect the driver you compiled yourself and the packaged one are conflicting in some way. Did you try uninstalling both and reinstalling just the packaged one as I suggested before?

bb002: Perhaps the problem here isn't an xorg.conf one (yet), since wacdump doesn't seem to be responding.

---

### Post by bb002 on 2005-10-31
I think xorg and wacdump are conflicting. I didn't notice it before because i didn't test for wacdump to be responding. I noticed that if i had the tablet lines in xorg, wacdump would see the tablet but would not pick up any of it's movements. Once i commented out the tablet lines in xorg.conf and restarted the pc, wacdump detected pen movments. Also it seems i do indeed have to use "wacdump -f c100 -c serial/dev/ttyS1" to get wacdump to see my tablet. thought i could leave the "-f c100" off before.

On my tablet the ReadRaw:  -1 (144) where the 144 is going up by one every second if the tablet is not detecting movement.

Sorry 23meg...I misread his post. Thought he had wacdump working.

### Post by 23meg on 2005-10-31
What I mean is, you first compiled the linux wacom driver yourself and installed it, didn't you? The files installed during that and those included in the xserver-xorg-input-wacom package may be conflicting. I don't know how to remove a self-compiled version of the driver, but that's what you should do. You  can probably find instructions on the linuxwacom driver site.

---

### Post by andrewsawyer on 2005-10-31
The wacom source 'thingy' (wacom.o) was copied across to the correct directory in the kernel source before I recompiled the source and started using that, however that resulted in my wireless dying.  So I uninstalled that kernel and I am currently using the standard, unchanged 686 kernel.  When installing the wacom-kernel-source, I notice that it compiles into the 686 headers, however having said that I've not recompiled the source since installing this download.

I have tried commenting out all references to the tablet in xorg.conf and rebooting, however it has made no difference I'm afraid. I guess I could try recompiliing the kernel now that the wacom-kernel-source has been installed.  That would be a last resort though, as it's a long job on my little laptop!

### Post by bb002 on 2005-10-31
as far as i understand wacdump gets the raw input of the tablet without any drivers. I'm wondering if your SetSerial command is causing a problem. I'm afraid I'm clueless on it's purpose.

---

### Post by bb002 on 2005-10-31
I downloaded setserial through synaptic.

I ran "setserial /dev/ttyS1" and I got back the following:
"/dev/ttyS1, UART: 16550A, Port: 0x02f8, IRQ: 3"

Dunno if that will help you any or not. It isn't making any sense that wacdump still doesn't respond to pen movement after commenting out all tablet related lines and restarting the laptop.

The WacomOpenTablet: Invalid Argument that you were getting before was just wacdump's way of telling you that /dev/ttyS0 wasn't a valid tablet device. Now that wacdump is starting, that center section should go nuts whenever you move the pen across the screen. Can you verify the pen itself is working?? can the laptop boot into windows? that way we know for sure both the pen and screen are still working. Or do you have another tablet pc you can go test the pen on? We might as rule out hardware as a possible problem. In any case it's one in the morning and i have to get to bed.

---

Sorry so long for the reply - I had to go away for a couple of days.

---

### Post by andrewsawyer on 2006-02-09
Did It!!!

/dev/wacom0.

Put these into both wacdump and it gave me output, then again into xorg.conf and it has worked.  I have to say I was very happy with it!  I hadn't touched on it for ages, however I've just set up my dad's machine and he uses a Wacom ArtpadII, which I got working with no trouble at all.  So figured I'd give mine another go.

Well, just to confim, if anyone can't get the tablet working, give /dev/wacom0 a try!

Cheers for all your help.

Andy

---

### Post by zappa86 on 2006-02-22
WOW! it does work. I was trying all the setserial stuff and going nowhere. I just added /dev/wacom0 as the device in my Xorg.conf and it was all nice and working. Thank you very much.

---
